description: Implement basic enemy AI with three behavior states: patrol (follow a path), chase (pursue player when detected), and attack (engage when in range). Two faction types for MVP: station defense bots and mutated crew.
acceptance_criteria: Given an enemy is in 'patrol' state, when it takes a turn, then it moves along a predefined path or wanders randomly within its room.
Given the player enters an enemy's detection radius AND is within their vision arc, when the enemy's turn processes, then the enemy transitions to 'chase' state.
Given an enemy is in 'chase' state, when it takes a turn, then it pathfinds toward the player's last known position using a simple A* or BFS algorithm.
Given an enemy is adjacent to the player (melee) or within weapon range (ranged), when it takes a turn, then it attacks the player.
Station defense bots are ranged attackers that maintain distance and prefer cover positions.
Mutated crew are melee-focused with erratic movement patterns (occasional random direction changes during chase).
If an enemy loses sight of the player for 3+ turns, it returns to patrol state at its last known player position.
spec_section: 7.3 Enemy Design Philosophy, 11.1 MVP Scope (Enemy AI)
notes: Spec: 'Station Defenses: Predictable, pattern-based. Hackable.' and 'Mutated Crew: Melee-focused, erratic movement. Some retain partial intelligence.' MVP = 2 factions, 3 states each.
